result:
I’d make it impossible for people to dehumanize each other.

Not force everyone to agree, not erase conflict, not make everyone “nice.” Just remove that mental switch that lets someone see another human as less real, less worthy, or disposable. A lot of the worst things—violence, exploitation, cruelty at scale—depend on that gap.

People could still argue, compete, even dislike each other. But they’d feel, on some level, “this person is as real as me.” That constraint alone would ripple through politics, economics, justice, even everyday interactions.

It wouldn’t solve everything. But it would make a lot of the worst outcomes much harder to justify—and harder to carry out.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
